examples: Objective To study the imaging characters of pericardial cysts.
Pericardial effusion is a collection of fluid in the pericardium.
Rarely pericardial effusion can be caused by infection and consist of pus.
Chronic pericarditis is usually associated with chronic inflammation and may result in fluid around the heart (pericardial effusion).
At times, it may be difficult to distinguish pericardial pain from the pain that occurs with a heart attack.
